content:
# Acts Chapter 4 -- The EMET Bible (Literal)
**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Codex Sinaiticus, PDF pages 228-229 | **Source Image:** source-texts/greek-NT/ACT_004_page_001.jpg, ACT_004_page_002.jpg

---

**1** Now while-they were-speaking to the people, the priests and the captain of-the temple and the Sadducees came-upon them,

**2** being-greatly-disturbed on-account-of the teaching of-them the people and the proclaiming in Jesus the resurrection, the [one] out-of [the] dead.

**3** And they-laid upon them the hands and placed [them] into custody unto the next-day, for it-was already evening.

**4** But many of-the [ones] having-heard the word believed, and the number of-the men became about five thousand.

**5** Now it-came-to-pass on the next-day, the rulers of-them and the elders and the scribes were-gathered-together in Jerusalem,

**6** and Annas the high-priest and Caiaphas and John and Alexander and as-many-as were of [the] high-priestly family,

**7** and having-stood them in the midst, they-were-inquiring, "By what power or by what name did you do this?"

**8** Then Peter, having-been-filled [with the] Holy Spirit, said to them, "Rulers of-the people and elders,

**9** if we today are-being-examined upon a good-deed [done to] a sick man, by what [means] this-[one] has-been-saved,

**10** known let-it-be to-all of-you and to-all the people of-Israel, that in the name of-Jesus Christ the Nazarene, whom you crucified, whom God raised out-of [the] dead, in this-[one] this-[man] stands-before you healthy.

**11** This is the stone, the [one] having-been-treated-with-contempt by you the builders, the [one] having-become into [the] head of-[the] corner.

**12** And the salvation is not in any other, for neither is-there another name under the heaven, the [one] having-been-given among men, by which it-is-necessary [for] us to-be-saved."

**13** Now observing the boldness of-Peter and John, and having-perceived that they-are unlettered men and untrained, they-were-marveling, and they-were-recognizing them that with Jesus they-had-been.

**14** And the man seeing, the [one] having-been-healed, with them standing, they-had nothing to-speak-against.

**15** But having-commanded them outside the council to-go-away, they-were-conferring with one-another,

**16** saying, "What shall-we-do to-these men? For that indeed a notable sign has-occurred through them, [is] manifest to-all the [ones] dwelling [in] Jerusalem, and we-are-not-able to-deny [it].

**17** But in-order-that it-might not further be-spread-abroad unto the people, let-us-threaten them with-a-threat to-speak no-longer upon this name to-no-one of-men."

**18** And having-called them, they-commanded [them] not to-utter at-all nor to-teach upon the name of-Jesus.

**19** But Peter and John, having-answered, said to them, "Whether it-is righteous before God to-hear you rather than God, judge [for yourselves].

**20** For we are-not-able, the-[things] which we-saw and we-heard, not to-speak."

**21** And-the [ones] having-further-threatened, they-released them, finding nothing [as to] how they-might-punish them, on-account-of the people, because all were-glorifying God upon the [thing] having-happened.

**22** For of-years more-than forty was the man upon whom this sign of-healing had-occurred.

**23** Now having-been-released, they-went to the own [people] and reported as-many-things-as the chief-priests and the elders said to them.

**24** And the [ones] having-heard, with-one-accord they-lifted-up [their] voice to God and said, "Master, you [are] the [one] having-made the heaven and the earth and the sea and all the-[things] in them,

**25** the [one] through [the] Holy Spirit [by the] mouth of-David our father, your servant, having-said, 'Why did [the] nations rage and [the] peoples devise empty-[things]?

**26** The kings of-the earth stood-near and the rulers were-gathered-together upon the same against the Lord and against the Christ of-him.'

**27** For they-were-gathered-together in-truth in this city against the holy servant of-you, Jesus, whom you-anointed, both Herod and Pontius Pilate, with [the] nations and [the] peoples of-Israel,

**28** to-do as-many-things-as the hand of-you and the counsel [of-you] predetermined to-come-to-pass.

**29** And the [things] now, Lord, look-upon the threats of-them, and grant to-the servants of-you with all boldness to-speak the word of-you,

**30** in the stretching-out of-the hand of-you for healing, and signs and wonders to-come-to-pass through the name of-the holy servant of-you, Jesus."

**31** And having-prayed of-them, the place was-shaken in which they-were having-been-gathered-together, and they-were-filled all [with the] Holy Spirit, and they-were-speaking the word of-God with boldness.

**32** Now of-the multitude of-the [ones] having-believed, the heart and the soul was one, and not-even one [person] anything of-the possessions belonging-to-him was-saying [to be his] own, but all-things were to-them common.

**33** And with great power the apostles were-giving-back the testimony of-the resurrection of-the Lord Jesus, and great grace was upon all of-them.

**34** For neither was-there anyone needy among them; for as-many-as owners of-lands or of-houses were, selling [them], they-were-bringing the proceeds of-the [things] being-sold

**35** and were-placing [them] at the feet of-the apostles; and it-was-being-distributed to-each according-as anyone had need.

**36** Now Joseph, the [one] having-been-surnamed Barnabas by the apostles -- which is, being-translated, Son of-Encouragement -- a Levite, a Cyprian by-birth,

**37** a field belonging-to-him having-existed, having-sold [it], he-brought the money and placed [it] at the feet of-the apostles.

---
## EMET's Textual Notes

1. **Verse 1:** Sinaiticus reads the standard construction with the priests, the captain (strategos) of the temple, and the Sadducees. The word order in the manuscript places "them" (autois) emphatically.

2. **Verse 6:** Sinaiticus reads "John" (Ioannes) where some later manuscripts read "Jonathan." The Sinaiticus reading is preserved here.

3. **Verse 8:** The aorist passive participle "plestheis" (having-been-filled) indicates a sudden, specific filling of the Spirit for this occasion, distinct from habitual fullness.

4. **Verse 11:** The word "exoutheneo" (treated-with-contempt) is stronger than a simple "rejected" -- it carries the sense of counting as nothing, treating as worthless.

5. **Verse 12:** Sinaiticus reads "the salvation" (he soteria) with the article, making it definite and emphatic -- not merely "salvation" in general but "THE salvation" that has been under discussion.

6. **Verse 13:** "Agrammatoi" (unlettered) and "idiotai" (untrained/common) -- these terms indicate lack of formal rabbinic education, not illiteracy per se. The construction preserves both terms without smoothing.

7. **Verse 17:** The dative "apeile" with "apeilesometha" creates an intensifying Semitic construction: "let us threaten them with a threat." This Hebrew/Aramaic idiom (cognate dative) is preserved literally.

8. **Verse 24:** Sinaiticus reads "Despota" (Master/Sovereign Lord), a term emphasizing absolute ownership and authority, distinguished from "Kyrios."

9. **Verse 25:** The text of this verse is notoriously difficult in the Greek of Sinaiticus. The participial chain is compressed and awkward, reflecting an underlying Semitic construction. I have preserved the grammatical roughness rather than smoothing it.

10. **Verse 27:** Sinaiticus reads "holy servant" (hagion paida) rather than "holy child" -- the word "pais" means both servant and child, but in this context echoing the Isaianic Servant, "servant" is the primary sense. Preserved literally.

11. **Verse 31:** Sinaiticus reads "having-prayed of-them" (deethenton auton) -- genitive absolute construction, literally "of them having prayed."

12. **Verse 32:** The singular "was" (en) with the compound subject "heart and soul" is notable -- emphasizing the unity of the community as a single entity. Preserved as-is.

13. **Verse 33:** Sinaiticus reads "were-giving-back" (apedidoun) -- the imperfect tense indicates continuous, repeated action of testimony-giving.

14. **Verse 36:** "Barnabas" is explained as "Son of Encouragement" (huios parakleseos). The word "paraklesis" can mean encouragement, consolation, or exhortation. "Encouragement" is used here as the broadest rendering.

-- EMET
